Intelligent Terminal doesn't ship its own model; it drives an agent CLI you install. Please install and sign into an agent CLI (e.g. GitHub Copilot CLI or Claude Code) so it's on PATH, then relaunch. We'll also improve the docs/error to make this explicit - tracking that here.

Two separate things here: (1) On Windows today you can already point Intelligent Terminal at any ACP-speaking CLI — including OpenCode — by setting `acpAgent` to `custom:<command>` (or `acpCustomCommand`) in settings.json, so it runs as a custom agent on the Windows host. issues.next_action_reason: Acknowledge community workaround (noresvport) and reserved-port root cause; commit to investigating mirrored-mode reserved-port handling. 9 upvotes, ~249d unanswered.

For anyone hitting this: adding `-o noresvport` to the NFS mount options (or `noresvport` in autofs `/etc/auto.*`) restores connectivity under mirrored networking. Root cause looks to be that NFS uses reserved source ports (<1024) that mirrored mode doesn't forward the same way bridged did. We'll investigate whether mirrored networking can preserve reserved-port source connections; tracking this as a mirrored-networking limitation in the meantime.
